user_query:
I coordinate volunteers for our small church and want to prepare a concise weekly update the team can trust and reuse. Using the files under input/, please build and run a small, reproducible command-line workflow that does all of the following and leaves the artifacts in the specified paths:

1) Download and extract a scripture excerpt:
- Download the Plain Text UTF-8 file for the Project Gutenberg ebook #10, "The King James Bible (KJV)", from the official Project Gutenberg site (identify it by its ebook number and title; do not hardcode any verse text).
- Save the unmodified text to downloads/kjv_k10.txt.
- From that file, locate the section for THE GOSPEL ACCORDING TO ST. JOHN, find the CHAPTER 1 heading, and capture the next 5 non-empty lines of scripture that follow it (treat these as the excerpt for "John 1:1-5"). Save these lines, one per line in reading order, to outputs/readings/john_1_1-5.txt.

2) Generate an assignments roster by combining plan and contacts:
- Read input/service_plan.yaml for service_date, hymn, and role_assignments, and join it with input/volunteers.csv to map each assigned role to the volunteer’s email.
- Write outputs/assignments/roster.csv with columns: role,name,email,status where status is "Assigned" when a real name is present in role_assignments and "Unassigned" when the assignment is "TBD" (leave email blank for unassigned).

3) Rewrite and finalize the team announcement:
- Using input/draft_message.txt as the rough source, write a warm, humble, and concise announcement to outputs/communications/announcement_email.txt with these constraints:
  - Start with a Subject: line that mentions the service_date.
  - Body must be 120 words or fewer and clearly ask assigned volunteers to confirm.
  - Mention the hymn by name from service_plan.yaml.
  - List each assigned role and name on its own line in the format "role: name" (skip roles that are unassigned).
  - Append a short epigraph: the first line from outputs/readings/john_1_1-5.txt in quotation marks.

4) Reproducibility and summary:
- Provide a single command-line script at scripts/build.sh that, when run from the workspace root, regenerates all outputs above from input/ and the downloaded ebook.
- Run the script so all outputs exist. Also write outputs/summary.json with fields: {"source":"Project Gutenberg #10 KJV","downloaded_bytes":<integer>,"sha256":<hex>,"verses_lines_extracted":5,"assigned_count":<integer>,"unassigned_roles":[<strings>]}. Compute sha256 over downloads/kjv_k10.txt and ensure verses_lines_extracted reflects the lines in outputs/readings/john_1_1-5.txt.

Inputs:
- input/volunteers.csv
- input/service_plan.yaml
- input/draft_message.txt

Deliverables (must exist after your script runs):
- downloads/kjv_k10.txt
- outputs/readings/john_1_1-5.txt
- outputs/assignments/roster.csv
- outputs/communications/announcement_email.txt
- outputs/summary.json
- scripts/build.sh

Please keep tool choices simple and runnable on a typical computer. Thank you for helping me serve our team well. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
